name: Vince Guaraldi
description: '''Vincent Anthony Guaraldi''' ({{IPAc-en|ɡ|ə|ˈ|r|æ|l|d|i}}; [[birth name|né]] '''Dellaglio''', July 17, 1928 – February 6, 1976) was an American [[jazz]] pianist who composed music for animated television adaptations of the ''[[Peanuts]]'' comic strip. His compositions for this series included their signature melody "[[Linus and Lucy]]" and the holiday standard "[[Christmas Time Is Here]]". Guaraldi also performed on piano as a member of [[Cal Tjader]]'s 1950s ensembles and for his own solo career. Guaraldi's 1962 composition "[[Cast Your Fate to the Wind]]" became a radio hit and won a [[Grammy Award]] in 1963 for [[Grammy Award for Best Original Jazz Composition|Best Original Jazz Composition]]. He died of a ruptured [[abdominal aortic aneurysm]] on February 6, 1976, at age 47, moments after concluding the first half of a nightclub performance in [[Menlo Park, California]].
